_meta.purpose: Data-only rules consumed by gen-opencode.py to port .claude/agents + .claude/commands/propuesta.md into .opencode/. Never edit generated output directly -- edit this file or the .claude/ sources, then re-run the generator.

frontmatter._note_drop_keys: name: OpenCode derives the agent id from the filename (1:1 with the 9 Claude filenames). argument-hint: no OpenCode markdown-command equivalent, per design's Dispatcher section.

model_map._assumption: Verified via `opencode models openai` on this machine (openai/gpt-5.4 present and available). Both Claude source model tiers (sonnet, opus) map to the same OpenCode model by explicit user choice -- the ported agents run on OpenAI GPT-5.4 rather than mirroring Claude's model tiering, since no anthropic provider is configured/authenticated locally.

permission_map._note: Keyed by the exact literal value of the source agent's `tools:` frontmatter field. `__no_tools_field__` is the sentinel for agents with no `tools:` key at all (full Claude default access). Script MUST fail loudly (never silently default) if a source file's `tools:` value is not a key here -- this enforces the spec's 'never narrow beyond the Claude original' requirement.

substitutions.find: El usuario quiere redactar una propuesta de investigación en IA siguiendo el
substitutions.replace:
**Nota de ejecución (solo OpenCode):** los gates de aprobación de este pipeline ("NO avances sin aprobación") requieren que la sesión de OpenCode se mantenga activa e interactiva entre fases -- este comando es resumible (el usuario responde en cada gate y el pipeline continúa dentro de la misma sesión), NO soporta una ejecución headless de una sola pasada (por ejemplo, `opencode run` de punta a punta sin sesión persistente), porque ese modo no puede detenerse a esperar la aprobación humana en cada gate.

El usuario quiere redactar una propuesta de investigación en IA siguiendo el
substitutions._note: Implements spec Requirement 'Interactive-Session-Only Constraint' (verify-report CRITICAL-1, obs #208): the source .claude/commands/propuesta.md's gate-wait prose is Claude-generic and never names OpenCode's headless-vs-interactive/resumable session distinction, so it must be injected explicitly into the generated artifact, not inferred. Uses the existing literal find/replace substitution mechanism as an insertion technique -- find matches the body's first line (verified single occurrence via rg) and replace prepends the note plus a blank line before that same first line, so the note lands at the very top of the body, right after frontmatter, before any pipeline content. Runs first in this array; the find target is disjoint from every other rule's find target so ordering relative to the other rules does not matter.

6 verified occurrences: investigador.md:56,68,116; revisor.md:53; presupuestador.md:25,81. Generic tool-neutral wording; does not touch the 47 'Task -> <agente>' flow arrows in propuesta.md (see propuesta-flow-arrows-unchanged note below).

substitutions.find: El Read tool de Claude Code no puede leer archivos `.docx` binarios
substitutions.replace: El Read tool no puede leer archivos `.docx` binarios de forma nativa en todos los runtimes (comportamiento no verificado caso por caso)
substitutions._note: Per spec Requirement 'insumos-observador .docx Claim Correction': OpenCode's own .docx Read-tool behavior is UNVERIFIED in this PR (open item, also flagged in design's Open Questions), so this rule takes the spec's tool-neutral rephrase fallback rather than asserting a specific corrected OpenCode capability claim. The textutil/unzip conversion fallback prose that follows is already tool-neutral and is left unchanged.

substitutions.find: de Claude Code no pueden invocar a otros subagentes. Usa la herramienta `Task`
substitutions.replace: de OpenCode no pueden invocar a otros subagentes. Usa la herramienta `task` (o la mención `@<agente>`)
substitutions._note: Must run before propuesta-task-tool-name so the generic backtick-Task rule does not need to (and does not) re-touch this already-lowercased occurrence.

substitutions.find: para despachar cada fase al subagente correspondiente en `.claude/agents/`.
substitutions.replace: para despachar cada fase al subagente correspondiente en `.opencode/agents/` (o mediante la mención `@<agente>`).
`coordinador-propuesta.md` (documento de referencia del pipeline, no incluido en este puerto)
substitutions._note: coordinador-propuesta.md is intentionally excluded from the port (see paths.excluded_agents); the reference is kept as a bare filename so it neither reintroduces a .claude/ path nor implies a .opencode/agents/coordinador-propuesta.md file that will never exist.

Catches the 4 remaining literal backtick-quoted tool-name mentions (original lines 29, 138, 772, 787) after propuesta-framing-claude-to-opencode has already consumed the line-11 occurrence. Order in this array matters -- apply sequentially.

DOCUMENTATION-ONLY entry, not an active rule (applies_to is empty on purpose so the engine skips it). Design decision: the 47 verified 'Task -> <agente>' flow arrows in propuesta.md's pipeline diagram stay UNCHANGED -- they are human-readable plan notation, not executable calls, and none of them contain a drift_patterns match ('Claude Code', 'Task tool', '.claude/', or backtick-Task), so leaving them as-is passes drift-lint cleanly.

_drift_patterns_note: First 3 are the design's baseline set. '`Task`' (backtick-quoted capital-T tool name) was added after auditing the real source files: it is the literal token the propuesta-task-tool-name substitution eliminates, and keeping it as a drift pattern is a safety net against a future source edit reintroducing a Claude-specific backtick-Task mention without a matching rule update.
open_items: model_map ids unverified against a live anthropic provider on this machine (see model_map._assumption).
insumos-observador.md .docx claim uses the spec's tool-neutral rephrase fallback, not a verified OpenCode-specific corrected claim (see docx-claude-code-claim._note).

`.mcp.json` mentions in bibliografo-propuesta.md and investigador.md are left untouched -- spec's Scope Boundary requirement says the generator must not touch .mcp.json itself; it does not require rewriting text that merely mentions the filename, and design does not list it as a substitution target or drift pattern. Flagged here for a human follow-up decision, not treated as in-scope drift for this PR.
